title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXI.
content:
[1]


    Observe when mother earth is dry,
    She drinks the droppings of the sky;
    And then the dewy cordial gives
    To every thirsty plant that lives.
    The vapors, which at evening weep,
    Are beverage to the swelling deep;
    And when the rosy sun appears,
    He drinks the ocean's misty tears.
    The moon too quaffs her paly stream
    Of lustre, from the solar beam.
    Then, hence with all your sober thinking!
    Since Nature's holy law is drinking;
    I'll make the laws of nature mine,
    And pledge the universe in wine.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXI."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
